calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-22-2026 Set For Trial On Nov-16-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion Of For Leave To Amend Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Jan-28-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT KIERAN CARROLL'S Motion Of For Leave To Amend Cross-Complaint.

Defendant Kieran O'Carroll dba Kieran O'Carroll Construction's motion for leave to amend O'Carroll's cross complaint to add a cause of action for breach of contract is granted. Defendant's added breach cause of action is a compulsory cross-complaint because it is alleged against the plaintiff and arises out of the same transactions or events that give rise to the complaint; if it is not pled as a cross-complaint, it is forfeit. (See Code Civ. Proc., sec. 426.30.) "A policy of liberal construction of [Code of Civil Procedure] section 426.50 to avoid forfeiture of causes of action is imposed on the trial court. A motion to file a cross-complaint at any time during the course of the action must be granted unless bad faith of the moving party is demonstrated where forfeiture would otherwise result. Factors such as oversight, inadvertence, neglect, mistake or other cause, are insufficient grounds to deny the motion unless accompanied by bad faith." (Silver Organizations Ltd. v. Frank (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 94, 98-99.) Plaintiffs would have the court find bad faith based on mere delay, but inadvertence is not bad faith, and the court does not have a basis on this record to find bad faith. Moreover, the court sees not prejudice. Trial has been set for November 2026, and there is ample time for discovery.

Plaintiffs further contend that the cross-complaint is futile because the statute of limitations bars the breach of contract cause of action. That issue may be raised through demurrer or summary judgment motion.

The court grants plaintiffs' request for judicial notice. O'Carroll shall file the amended pleading submitted with its motion by February 9, 2026.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
